items.question: What's the primary value of integration tests over unit tests?
options: they catch wiring/contract bugs that mocked unit tests can't see
items.answer: they catch wiring/contract bugs that mocked unit tests can't see
items.explanation: Unit tests mock the boundary — exactly where real bugs often live.

items.question: Which is the canonical tool for spinning up real databases in Python integration tests?
items.explanation: testcontainers-python runs real Postgres/Redis/Kafka in Docker per test session.

items.question: Why pin Docker image versions in integration tests?
options: to avoid silent upstream changes that flake CI
items.answer: to avoid silent upstream changes that flake CI
items.explanation: `latest` resolves to whatever the registry serves at pull time — non-deterministic.

items.question: VCR cassettes are a good fit when:
options: you need the latest provider behaviour every run
you want deterministic, fast, free replay of stable HTTP exchanges
the API has no fixed schema
items.answer: you want deterministic, fast, free replay of stable HTTP exchanges
items.explanation: Cassettes replay recorded responses; re-record on schedule to detect drift.

items.question: The right reaction to a flaky integration test is:
options: investigate the root cause (shared state, race, real time)
items.answer: investigate the root cause (shared state, race, real time)
items.explanation: Retries hide real bugs that hit production at scale.

items.question: How do most teams keep the local feedback loop fast despite a large integration suite?
items.explanation: Engineers run the fast suite locally; CI runs the integration suite.
